_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of testing, evaluation, validation, and verification (T&E V&V) tools and procedures to ensure systems are working as intended.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing automated tests for data applications, including unit tests for transformation logic, integration tests for pipeline components, and data validation tests using appropriate testing frameworks.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-002 (RM, cosine: 0.542), RC-K-001 (RC, cosine: 0.522).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.692). Shared vocabulary: [testing, validation]. DCWF-only terms [ensure, evaluation, intended, knowledge, procedures, systems] vs WIDAI-only terms [applications, appropriate, automated, components, data, frameworks] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.846, R=0.871). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 27 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 19 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Knowledge of testing, evaluation, validation, and verification (T&E V&V) tools a" and "Skill in writing automated tests for data applications, including unit tests for" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.548 < STS 0.692 (gap -0.144). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ensure, evaluation, intended] and [applications, appropriate, automated] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.1%. Shared tokens: [testing, validation]. DCWF-only: [ensure, evaluation, intended, knowledge, procedures, systems]. WIDAI-only: [applications, appropriate, automated, components, data, frameworks].
